title: Fix Settings Page Unauthorized Error for Unauthenticated Users

requirements.summary: Fix the Settings page to not attempt backend calls when user is not authenticated and show a login prompt instead
acceptanceCriteria: When a user is not authenticated, the Settings page does not call the backend settings endpoint.
When a user is not authenticated, the Settings page displays a message prompting the user to log in rather than an error.
When a user is authenticated, the Settings page loads settings successfully without any 'Unauthorized' error.
No 'Failed to load settings' error message appears for authenticated users.

file_operations.description: Add authentication check before rendering settings content. If the user is not authenticated (no identity from useInternetIdentity), display the AccountsDisabledNotice component to prompt login instead of attempting to load settings. Only render the settings form and call useAccountSettings hooks when the user is authenticated. Ensure proper loading states are shown while checking authentication status.

requirements.summary: Fix the useAccountSettings hook to guard all backend calls with authentication checks
acceptanceCriteria: The useAccountSettings hook does not issue a backend request when no authenticated session is present.
The hook returns a clear unauthenticated state (e.g., disabled query, null data) when the user is not logged in.
The hook fetches and mutates settings correctly when the user is authenticated.

file_operations.description: Guard all React Query hooks in useAccountSettings with authentication checks. For useGetSettings, add an 'enabled' condition that checks both actor availability and authentication status (from useInternetIdentity) to prevent the query from running when unauthenticated. For all mutation hooks (updateDisplayName, updateAvatar, updateVisibility, updateLanguage, deleteAccount), add early validation to throw a descriptive error or return early if actor is not available or user is not authenticated. Ensure the query returns a disabled/idle state with null data when authentication requirements are not met.
